title: 10 medicinal constituents of Populus x candicans (bastard cottonwood)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Populus × candicans salicin is a unique compound found in its bark. It belongs to the Salicaceae family and is characterized by its distinct white or yellowish coloration. This substance has been used medicinally for centuries due to its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to alleviate symptoms of various illnesses.

Populus × candicans phenolic acids is a group of compounds found in its bark and leaves. These acids are responsible for the tree's defense mechanisms against pathogens and insects, providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. They have been isolated and characterized, displaying potential applications in medicine, agriculture, and biotechnology, making Populus × candicans an important source of natural products with valuable biological activities.

Populus × candicans flavonoids is a class of plant compounds that have been identified in its bark and leaves. These flavonoids have been shown to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, they have been found to exhibit potential therapeutic effects against certain diseases, such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.

Populus × candicans catechins is a type of bioactive compound found in its leaves and bark. These flavonoid compounds have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Research suggests that they may help protect against diseases such as cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the growth of pathogens. Further studies are needed to fully understand their potential health benefits.

Populus × candicans quercetin is a flavonoid compound that has been isolated from its bark. This bioactive compound exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to the tree's natural defense against pathogens and pests. Quercetin has also been found to possess anti-cancer and antimicrobial activities, making it a promising candidate for further research in medicine and agriculture.

Populus × candicans kaempferol is a flavonoid compound found in the leaves and stems of this plant. It has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases. Kaempferol has also been linked to improved cardiovascular health and may have protective effects against cancer cells. Further research is needed to fully explore its benefits and potential applications.

Populus × candicans isoflavones is a type of phytochemical compound found in the leaves and bark of this plant. These compounds have been shown to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. They have also been reported to exhibit potential medicinal effects, such as lowering cholesterol levels and improving blood sugar control, making them a subject of interest for further research and potential therapeutic applications.

Populus × candicans tannins is a group of complex organic compounds found in the bark and leaves of this tree species. These tannins are responsible for its characteristic bitterness and astringency, which have been used traditionally to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. They also impart a dark color and astringent taste to the wood, making it useful for woodworking and other applications.

Populus × candicans lignans is a group of organic compounds found in its bark and leaves. These lignans have been studied for their potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Research has shown that they may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders.

Populus × candicans sesquiterpenes is a type of compound found in its leaves and stems. These natural compounds have been shown to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Research has also demonstrated that they exhibit potential therapeutic effects against certain diseases, such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders.

intro: Populus × candicans has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. For example, the flavonoids may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, while the terpenoids could exhibit antiviral activity against respiratory infections. Additionally, the phenolic acids might display antibacterial properties, aiding in wound healing and reducing the risk of infection.
intro_desc: Populus × candicans has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These constituents possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. They can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, by soothing irritated airways. Furthermore, the antioxidants in hybrid black cottonwood can also protect against cell damage, potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases, and promote overall cardiovascular health.
